texto: Hope is more the consequence of action than its cause. As the experience of the spectator favors fatalism, so the experience of the agent produces hope.
explicacao: Citação de Roberto Mangabeira Unger sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Hope is more the consequence of action than its cause. As the experience of the spectator favors …” articula reflexao, esperanca, experience em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

texto: So we must run back and forth between these two suns in our firmament—the presentiment of death and awareness of life—and avoid being transfixed by either of them. If we are lucky in this uncertain middle distance, we may form attachments and projects that enhance the sentiment of life. However, even as we try our luck, death comes to us, and brings our experiment to a end.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Roberto Mangabeira Unger sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. A afirmação “So we must run back and forth between these two suns in our firmament—the presentiment of death a…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, death, existentialism, útil em momentos de luto e termino.

texto: The combination of our mortality with our groundlessness imparts to human life its pressing and enigmatic character. We struggle to in our brief time in the midst of an impenetrable darkness. A small area is lighted up: our civilizations, our sciences, our loves. We prove unable to define the place of the lighted area within a larger space devoid of light, and must go to our deaths unenlightened.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Roberto Mangabeira Unger sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. A afirmação “The combination of our mortality with our groundlessness imparts to human life its pressing and e…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, death, existentialism, útil em momentos de luto e reflexao-noturna.

texto: In such a view, time is not emergent. It is, in fact, the only aspect of reality that cannot emerge from a more fundamental background. We register its reality, always and everywhere, by recognizing the differential character of change: some things change relative to other things. However, the kinds of things that there are also change, and so do the ways in which they change. That is what time is: the transformation of transformation.
explicacao: Citação de Roberto Mangabeira Unger sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “In such a view, time is not emergent. It is, in fact, the only aspect of reality that cannot emer…” articula reflexao, time, change em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como mudanca e reflexao.

texto: The embodied self is the same person who woke to the world in a burst of visonary immediacy, who soon found that he was not the center of that world but on the contrary, a dependent and even hapless creature, and who then discovered that he was doomed to die

texto: Insofar we are death-bound, existence is urgent and frightful. Insofar as are groundless, it is vertiginous and dreamlike. Insofar as we are insatiable, it is unquiet and tormented.
explicacao: Citação de Roberto Mangabeira Unger sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Insofar we are death-bound, existence is urgent and frightful.
